claims.text: Eligible when Customer on Pro plan trial forgot to cancel; charge of $49.00 processed 1 day after the 7-day trial ended. System confirms trial_end_date and charge_date delta ≤ 24 hours, no prior courtesy refund on account. Agent issues full $49.00 refund, cancels subscription, reverts to free plan, and states refund appears in 3–5 business days. Directly supported by src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262670462979.

Ineligible when Customer requests refund but system records show charge_date is 48 hours after trial_end_date. This exceeds the 24-hour post-trial window required for autonomous courtesy refund eligibility. Agent cannot issue refund unilaterally; must escalate to manager.

Escalate when Customer requests refund on a $99 annual renewal charge 2 days after renewal date; account shows zero logins in 11 months. This is not a trial-to-paid conversion and falls outside the trial courtesy refund policy. Per src_slack_c0ba1v2k269_1781262784193319, escalate for manager decision on partial or full refund options.

Ineligible when Charge is within 24 hours of trial end and customer qualifies on timing, but system records show a prior courtesy refund was already issued on this account under this same policy. The one-time-only nature of the courtesy refund disqualifies the request. Agent denies autonomous refund and escalates.

Escalate when Customer claims trial ended yesterday and requests refund for today's charge, but system records show no matching trial_end_date or the subscription record is ambiguous. Agent cannot confirm the 24-hour window without verified system data. Escalate for manual review before any refund action is taken.

Escalate when Trial ended and charge occurred within the 24-hour window, but the charge_amount provided by the customer ($79) does not match the subscription_plan_name pricing on record ($49). Discrepancy prevents confident full-refund issuance. Escalate to verify correct charge amount before processing.

evidence.retrieval_snippet: *[4/20 · Eligible]* I signed up for the Pro plan trial 6 days ago and forgot to cancel before the 7-day window closed. I was just charged $49/month. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude The charge went through just 1 day after your trial ended totally understandable to miss. As a one-time courtesy, I can issue a full refund of $49.00 and cancel your subscription immediately. Your account will revert to the free plan. The refund will appear in 35 business days. Want me to go ahead? *Sent using* Claude

*[13/20 · Edge Case]* I just got charged $99 for an annual renewal on a subscription I totally forgot I had. I haven't logged into the account in 11 months. Can I get a full refund? *Sent using* Claude I can see your account has had zero logins in the past 11 months and the renewal processed just 2 days ago. Our policy doesn't guarantee refunds on renewals, but given the clear non-usage history I can offer: (A) a 50% refund ($49.50) + immediate cancellation, or (B) a full $99.00 refund + account termination, which requires manager approval (~24hrs). Which would you prefer? *Sent using* Claude

*[14/20 · Edge Case]* I returned my order 3 weeks ago and got a return confirmation email, but I still haven't seen the refund on my credit card. Order #ORD-39201. What's going on? *Sent using* Claude I'm so sorry for the delay! The return for Order #ORD-39201 was confirmed on our end 21 days ago and the refund was issued. At this point it should have appeared it may show under a slightly different merchant name or as a pending credit. I'm escalating to our payments team to trace the transaction; they typically resolve these within 48 hours and I'll follow up directly. Can you confirm the last 4 digits of the card used? *Sent using* Claude

*[4/20 · Eligible]* I signed up for the Pro plan trial 6 days ago and forgot to cancel before the 7-day window closed. I was just charged $49/month. Can I get a refund? *Sent using* Claude The charge went through just 1 day after your trial ended totally understandable to miss. As a one-time courtesy, I can issue a full refund of $49.00 and cancel your subscription immediately. Your account will revert to the free plan. The refund will appear in 35 business days. Want me to go ahead? *Sent using* Claude

*[13/20 · Edge Case]* I just got charged $99 for an annual renewal on a subscription I totally forgot I had. I haven't logged into the account in 11 months. Can I get a full refund? *Sent using* Claude I can see your account has had zero logins in the past 11 months and the renewal processed just 2 days ago. Our policy doesn't guarantee refunds on renewals, but given the clear non-usage history I can offer: (A) a 50% refund ($49.50) + immediate cancellation, or (B) a full $99.00 refund + account termination, which requires manager approval (~24hrs). Which would you prefer? *Sent using* Claude

*[14/20 · Edge Case]* I returned my order 3 weeks ago and got a return confirmation email, but I still haven't seen the refund on my credit card. Order #ORD-39201. What's going on? *Sent using* Claude I'm so sorry for the delay! The return for Order #ORD-39201 was confirmed on our end 21 days ago and the refund was issued. At this point it should have appeared it may show under a slightly different merchant name or as a pending credit. I'm escalating to our payments team to trace the transaction; they typically resolve these within 48 hours and I'll follow up directly. Can you confirm the last 4 digits of the card used? *Sent using* Claude

runtime_profiles.install_instructions: Register this skill under the 'refund_eligibility' skill slug. Ensure the runtime has read access to subscription billing records including trial_end_date, charge_date, charge_amount, subscription_plan_name, and prior courtesy refund history. Grant write access to initiate refunds, cancel subscriptions, and update account plan status. Configure escalation routing to the billing manager queue for out-of-window or ambiguous cases. Set the one-time courtesy refund flag as a checkable account attribute to prevent duplicate issuance.
runtime_profiles.test_instructions: To validate this skill in a staging environment: (1) Seed a test account with trial_end_date = T and charge_date = T+20 hours; invoke skill and assert full refund issued, subscription cancelled, free plan restored. (2) Seed a second account with charge_date = T+30 hours; invoke and assert escalation path triggered, no refund issued. (3) Seed a third account with a prior courtesy refund flag set and charge_date = T+10 hours; invoke and assert ineligible response with escalation. (4) Seed an annual renewal scenario (no trial record); invoke and assert needs_escalation output matching manager-approval flow. (5) Seed an account with missing trial_end_date in system; invoke and assert escalation triggered before any refund action. Confirm all refund amounts in passing tests equal the seeded charge_amount exactly.

tests.name: Charge exactly 1 day (within 24 hours) after trial end — standard eligible case
Charge 48 hours after trial end — outside 24-hour window
Annual renewal charge with zero account usage — not a trial conversion
Customer has already received a prior courtesy refund on same account
Customer-stated trial end date conflicts with system records — unverifiable window
Charge within 24 hours but charge_amount does not match plan pricing on record
